context_rules.rule: Simple circuits (<=10 components): no labels needed, wire everything directly
context_rules.evidence: 040221-D, DE2010070162, 191207-02 — zero labels, all direct wires

context_rules.rule: MCU systems with many pins: use labels for port connections to avoid clutter
context_rules.evidence: 040218-D, DE2008070161, DE2008070201, DE2010070081 — heavy label use for MCU pins

context_rules.rule: Medium circuits (10-25 components): labels only for power nets, everything else direct
Complex multi-IC systems: label all inter-IC connections, wire only intra-block

label_vs_wire.summary: 85% of Elektor schematics use direct wires as dominant connection method. Labels average 3.1 per schematic, almost exclusively for power rails. Only MCU-heavy designs use labels extensively.

wire_routing.notes: No Elektor schematic in our sample used bus notation. All used individual wires or labels.
